spec_match.rationale: All preconditions enumerated (secure context, IWA scheme, Permissions Policy, API surface, Screen Details, and enterprise allowlist) are the same gates documented by the spec and ChromeStatus intent.

interactive.rationale: Probe button enumerates each precondition as a tile, try-capture actually invokes the API using the draft's sequence-of-MediaStreams shape, and the log shows rejection details.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'tile-by-tile gate inspection: find the missing precondition fast' and that is exactly the deliverable.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now uses `document.permissionsPolicy` when available, falls back to `document.featurePolicy`, and explicitly marks enterprise policy as an out-of-band chrome://policy check.

references.rationale: See also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 and the Capture All Screens spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, cell.ok / cell.bad both set background + color → no light-on-light regression.

summary: The policy probe now includes canonical references, uses the modern Permissions Policy interface when available, and explicitly tells users to verify the enterprise allowlist outside page JavaScript.
